macro_segments.title: The Obligation to Violate Shabbat for Witnesses to Testify About the New Moon

micro_segments.title: Whether the moon was seen clearly or unclearly, witnesses violate Shabbat to testify
Rabbi Yosi's objection: if clearly seen, others will testify without violating Shabbat
Rabban Gamliel's response: policy grounds require accepting all witnesses to ensure proper Kiddush Hachodesh

macro_segments.title: The Meaning of Be'alil (Clearly) and Textual Sources

micro_segments.title: Linguistic meaning of be'alil as clarity and refinement from Psalms 12:7
Rav and Shmuel debate on fifty gates of wisdom and Kohelet's aspiration
Kohelet wanted to judge by inner thoughts rather than testimony; heavenly voice rejected this

macro_segments.title: The Story of Shazpar and Rabbi Akiva Detaining the Witnesses

micro_segments.title: Forty pairs of witnesses on their way to testify; Rabbi Akiva held them up at Lod
Braita clarification: Shazpar (not Rabbi Akiva) detained them and was deposed
Discussion of whether violating Shabbat applies only to day 30 (29-day months)

macro_segments.title: Who Can Testify: Father and Son, Relatives, and Valid Witnesses

micro_segments.title: Father and son may both come to testify; not combining as one witness but as backup
Rabbi Shimon says all relatives are kosher for new moon testimony
Story of Tuvyah the doctor: kohanim accepted him and his son, rejected the freed slave
Beit Din accepted Tuvyah and the freed slave, rejected his son for being relatives
Subtext: kohanim value lineage, rabbis reject it; freed slave status was the priestly concern

macro_segments.title: Rav Levi's Source for Allowing Relatives: Moshe and Aharon at the Exodus

micro_segments.title: Verse: God commanded Moshe and Aharon about the month; implies brothers/relatives can testify
Rabbanan counter: the verse means leadership responsibility, not that relatives qualify
Discussion of whether new moon testimony is a classic event-testimony or statement of fact
Rav Lichtenstein: not about events vs. facts, but testimony 'against' someone per Torah

macro_segments.title: Rav Huna's Challenge to Rav Chana Bar Abba on the Halacha

micro_segments.title: Rav Chana rules like Rabbi Shimon (relatives valid); Rav Huna questions this against Rabbi Yosi's story
Rav Chana claims he said it in front of Rav many times and Rav never objected
Rav Huna: but you stated it backwards—Rabbi Shimon said relatives invalid, Rabbi Yosi valid
Conclusion: Tavi bar Mari reports in name of Shmuel—halacha like Rabbi Shimon (ambiguity remains)

macro_segments.title: Invalid Witnesses: The Categories of Disqualified Testifiers

micro_segments.title: List of invalid witnesses: dice players, usurers, pigeon racers, Shemitta traders, slaves
General rule: any testimony for which a woman is invalid, these groups are also invalid
Women are valid only to testify a husband died; all invalid witnesses also valid for that testimony
Rav Ashi: only rabbinic gazlan disqualified from wife-testimony; biblical gazlan always invalid
Discussion: why are biblical violations (interest, Shemitta) considered only rabbinic gazlan?
Tosafot and Rashi explanations: not biblical severity but conscious rationalization or not outright theft

macro_segments.title: Practical Provisions for Witnesses Traveling to Testify

micro_segments.title: Witnesses who cannot walk are carried on donkeys, even in bed, on Shabbat
If wayfarers threaten them, they may carry weapons; food and supplies for long journeys provided
Travel must be possible within night and day to arrive before nightfall same day
Verse: these are the holidays of God to declare in their time, justifies Shabbat violation

macro_segments.title: Unrecognized Witnesses and the Need for Character Witnesses

micro_segments.title: If Beit Din does not recognize witnesses, they send another person to vouch for them
Gemara debate: does mishna mean one character witness or another pair?
Gemara: even one character witness would suffice per simple text
Pappa's reading: another pair needed because mishpat (normal court rules) applies
Story of Rabbi Nehorai violating Shabbat alone to be character witness for someone else
Gemara harmonizes: Rabbi Nehorai relied on second witness already in Usha

macro_segments.title: Reports from Eretz Yisrael vs. Witnesses: Different Standards of Trustworthiness

micro_segments.title: Ulla reports sanctification happened in Eretz Yisrael; Rav Kahana: we trust any report, not just Ulla
Principle: any message that will be revealed cannot be successfully lied about
Braita: anyone from end of world reporting Beit Din sanctified month is believed
Distinction: witnesses need verification of identity; reporters rely on eventual revelation

macro_segments.title: The Baitusim Conspiracy: Attempted Deception of the Rabbis

micro_segments.title: Originally Beit Din accepted new moon testimony from anyone
Baitusim hired two men for 400 zuz to give false moon testimony
One hired man was secretly pro-rabbis; he exposed the plot with absurd testimony
Absurd description: moon crouching, with calf head, goat ears, deer horns, tail between thighs
Witness admits: I was paid 200 zuz to lie; Baitusim wanted to deceive you
Rabbis kept the 200 zuz, gave the hiring agent lashes, instituted recognition-only policy

macro_segments.title: The Bonfire System of Communication and Its Sabotage

micro_segments.title: Originally: mountaintop beacons (musuot) communicated Rosh Chodesh across Eretz Yisrael
Kusim (or Baitusim) interfered by lighting false fires; message system was hacked
Physical setup: cedar poles, twigs, olive wood, flax; waved up-down to signal clearly
Chain of mountains: Mount of Olives to Sartaba to Grofina to Havran to Beit Baltin
Final mountain illuminated all the exile; system covered hundreds of miles instantly
Result of sabotage: switched to slower messenger system to prevent further hacking

topical_tags.term: Fifty gates of wisdom (Nun sha'arei bina)
